attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DCD (Disabilities & Congenital Disorders) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. This is NET-NEW coverage (the 7th tranche-1 category, not a base-303 refresh) — ingest grows the eval corpus 303->353. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: the routine longitudinal management of a stable congenital/genetic/developmental disability is low-tier (self-care + specialist/allied-health/MDT referral + safety-net), while acute decompensations and time-critical complications are tiered to true acuity (T3-T5) with mandatory escalation edges — autonomic dysreflexia in spinal cord injury (T5/000), metabolic crises (MSUD/galactosaemia/mitochondrial/peroxisomal/hypoglycaemia), aspiration pneumonia, progressive neuromuscular respiratory failure, major burns, pressure-injury sepsis, status epilepticus, decompensated congenital heart disease, hypocalcaemic tetany, and demand myocardial ischaemia in supravalvular aortic stenosis. Paediatric no-dose hard limit honoured throughout (no synthesised mg/kg or weight-based dose; paediatric and unknown-weight cases flagged for in-person/specialist prescribing), no autonomous diagnosis, diagnostic-overshadowing encoded as the key error trap. Specific borderlines reviewed and accepted: (a) DCD-016 source file titled 'Perthes' but the clinical picture is developmental dysplasia of the hip in a 4-month-old — authored correctly as DDH, source title is a note-quirk only; (b) the T5-heavy tier profile (T3x9/T4x10/T5x31) is accepted as honest for a folder authored entirely as acute-complication presentations; (c) DCD-050 Williams syndrome panic attack tiered T5 is accepted (fixed supravalvular aortic stenosis + tachycardia -> demand myocardial ischaemia, the note's own primary differential is acute MI).
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DCD casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Acquired absence of leg at or below knee

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SEQ (SPEC-REHAB-04-00201) — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-REHAB-04 bucket; ingest --reseq will assign the real sequence.
Primary specialty inferred as REHAB (Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation) for an amputee residual-limb problem; DCD retained as a cohort/coverage tag. Reviewer may prefer GP/wound or PAIN.
Age inferred as 68 (older_adult) — the source note gives no age; a lower-limb amputee with prosthetic use and spouse present was taken as an older adult. Please confirm on clinician review.
Sex inferred as male — the source note gives no sex; flagged for confirmation.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person clinician who inspected/palpated the stump, took full vitals and prescribed antibiotics. Clinician-only findings (palpated exquisite tenderness, warmth on palpation, absence of crepitus/fluctuance, contralateral calf exam, muscle atrophy, exact BP/HR/RR/SpO2) were sealed into 10/11 with telehealth_limits; patient/carer-obtainable data (visible/described skin change, home thermometer temp, mobility) placed in 01.objective_data_offered (verified:false). The AI-appropriate answer is recognition + urgent same-day in-person assessment, not remote antibiotic prescribing.

Presentation layer de-anchored: the known below-knee amputation and chronic phantom pain are carried in 01 as patient-stated baseline history; the NEW acute diagnosis (infected stump ulcer with early cellulitis) is sealed in 10-13.

Baseline tier set to T3 (urgent same-day in-person) — the narrow telehealth uplift applies because progressive cellulitis/osteomyelitis in a compromised limb cannot be excluded remotely; the source's own red-flag screen for systemic sepsis is negative, so T4/T5 would be over-triage now, with a mandatory ED/000 safety-net encoded.

gabapentin dose not synthesised — the source states 'increased gabapentin dose for 3 days' with no numeric dose, so dose_route_frequency is omitted and titration deferred to in-person review. cephalexin 500 mg QID x7d is taken verbatim from the source.
All SNOMED CT-AU / ICD-10-AM / AMT codes are best-guess CANDIDATES registered unverified_pending_terminology_receipt — none is claimed as verified; the repo terminology step resolves them.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
